verified.description: American software company offering Cursor, an AI-assisted software development service. Founded in 2022, the company achieved a $29.3 billion valuation and surpassed $1 billion in annual recurring revenue by late 2025.
verified.notes: Total raised calculated from: $8M seed (Oct 2023) + $60M Series A + $900M Series C (June 2025) + $2.3B Series D (Nov 2025) = $3.268B. Wikipedia source [1] is most recent and comprehensive. Source [2] is about Cognition (different company), not Anysphere.

changesProposed.name: OpenAI

current.name: OpenAI
current.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

verified.name: OpenAI
verified.description: American artificial intelligence research organization consisting of a for-profit public benefit corporation and nonprofit foundation. Developer of the GPT family of large language models, DALL-E series of text-to-image models, and Sora series of text-to-video models.
verified.notes: Wikipedia lists 11 founders including Elon Musk (database entry lists only 3). In October 2025, OpenAI conducted a $6.6 billion share sale valued at $500 billion per source [1]. Company transitioned to for-profit capped entity in 2019 and underwent 2025 restructuring converting subsidiary into PBC. Microsoft ownership is 27% per [1]. Revenue was US$13.1 billion in 2025 per [1].

changesProposed.name: Scale AI

verified.name: Scale AI
verified.description: An artificial intelligence infrastructure and software company offering data annotation, RLHF services, LLM evaluation, and enterprise software suites to build and deploy AI applications. The company works with commercial clients including Google, Microsoft, and Meta, as well as with the United States Department of Defense on military-related projects.
verified.notes: In June 2025, Meta Platforms acquired a 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI for $14.8 billion. Founder Alexandr Wang left Scale AI to join Meta and was replaced as CEO by Jason Droege. The most recent valuation cited in sources is $14 billion as of May 2024.

changesProposed.notes: In June 2025, Meta Platforms acquired a 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI for $14.8 billion. Founder Alexandr Wang left Scale AI to join Meta and was replaced as CEO by Jason Droege. The most recent valuation cited in sources is $14 billion as of May 2024.
